segments.text: My son, keep my words and store up my commands within you.
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Father

segments.text: Keep my commands and you will live; guard my teachings as the apple of your eye.
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Father

segments.text: Bind them on your fingers; write them on the tablet of your heart.
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Father

segments.text: Say to wisdom, 'You are my sister,' and to insight, 'You are my relative.'
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Father

segments.text: They will keep you from the adulterous woman, from the wayward woman with her seductive words.
At the window of my house I looked down through the lattice.
I saw among the simple, I noticed among the young men, a youth who had no sense.
He was going down the street near her corner, walking along in the direction of her house
at twilight, as the day was fading, as the dark of night set in.
Then out came a woman to meet him, dressed like a prostitute and with crafty intent.
(She is unruly and defiant, her feet never stay at home;
now in the street, now in the squares, at every corner she lurks.)
She took hold of him and kissed him and with a brazen face she said:
Today I fulfilled my vows, and I have food from my fellowship offering at home.
segments.speaker: Adulterous Woman

segments.text: So I came out to meet you; I looked for you and have found you!
segments.speaker: Adulterous Woman

segments.text: I have covered my bed with colored linens from Egypt.
segments.speaker: Adulterous Woman

segments.text: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes and cinnamon.
segments.speaker: Adulterous Woman

segments.text: Come, let's drink deeply of love till morning; let's enjoy ourselves with love!
segments.speaker: Adulterous Woman

segments.text: My husband is not at home; he has gone on a long journey.
segments.speaker: Adulterous Woman

segments.text: He took his purse filled with money and will not be home till full moon.
segments.speaker: Adulterous Woman

segments.text: With persuasive words she led him astray; she seduced him with her smooth talk.
All at once he followed her like an ox going to the slaughter, like a deer stepping into a noose
till an arrow pierces his liver, like a bird darting into a snare, little knowing it will cost him his life.
Now then, my sons, listen to me; pay attention to what I say.
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Father

segments.text: Do not let your heart turn to her ways or stray into her paths.
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Father

segments.text: Many are the victims she has brought down; her slain are a mighty throng.
Her house is a highway to the grave, leading down to the chambers of death.
